reviewer: Claude (Principal Systems Architect — cross-repo ecosystem audit stance)
review_scope: Full cross-repository architectural audit of the 8-repo distributed ecosystem

actions.title: Remove production implementation code from the constitution repo
actions.description: The spectrum_systems/ Python package contains a full production study runner pipeline (pipeline.py, run_study.py, artifact_writer.py, load_config.py) and a contract loading utility. This directly violates the repo's stated boundary rule: 'This repository should NOT contain production implementation code.' The test suite (test_contracts.py) imports from spectrum_systems.contracts, coupling CI to the production code. The artifact boundary CI check does not detect Python source files.
actions.recommended_action: Relocate spectrum_systems/study_runner/ to a dedicated engine repo (e.g., spectrum-study-compiler-engine). Evaluate whether spectrum_systems/contracts/__init__.py belongs in a narrow published SDK or can be replaced with direct JSON schema loading. Refactor test_contracts.py to not import from a production Python package. Remove run_study.py from the repo root.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Remove production Python package from governance repo (spectrum_systems/)

actions.title: Complete the ecosystem registry with all operational engines
actions.description: ecosystem/ecosystem-registry.json lists 4 of 8 known ecosystem repos. Missing: working-paper-review-engine, comment-resolution-engine, meeting-minutes-engine, docx-comment-injection-engine. These are the primary Layer 3 consumers of the contract czar model. The registry also lacks compliance_status, governance_version, and contract_pins fields, providing no machine-readable governance state per repo.
actions.recommended_action: Add all four missing operational engines to ecosystem/ecosystem-registry.json with accurate repo_type, status, and description. Add compliance_status (compliant/pending/non-compliant/unknown), governance_version, and contract_pins fields per entry. Add a CI check validating registry completeness against a declared expected-repos list. Update ecosystem/ecosystem-registry.schema.json to reflect the new fields.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Add missing operational engine repos to ecosystem-registry.json with compliance fields

actions.title: Extend artifact boundary CI to enforce architecture boundary
actions.description: scripts/check_artifact_boundary.py enforces a data boundary (no .pdf, .docx, binary blobs) but does not enforce the architecture boundary (no production implementation code). Python source packages, runnable pipeline scripts, and business logic modules pass through CI unchallenged. The stated rule and the enforced rule are misaligned.
actions.recommended_action: Extend check_artifact_boundary.py to detect: (1) Python packages with __init__.py containing non-trivial business logic, (2) root-level runnable scripts matching run_*.py pattern, (3) any src/ directory. Define an allowlist for governance utilities (scripts/, tests/). Document the boundary distinction in docs/implementation-boundary.md.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Extend artifact-boundary CI to enforce architecture boundary, not only data boundary

actions.title: Activate Phase 1 governance enforcement: declared identity and contract pins
actions.description: The governance enforcement roadmap defines 4 phases but no phase is active. Phase 1 (declared identity and contract pins) is the prerequisite for all subsequent enforcement. Without it, downstream engines have no machine-enforceable obligation to declare system_id or pin contract versions. The governance/repo-compliance.schema.json exists but is never instantiated against any repo.
actions.recommended_action: Define a system-manifest.json schema requiring system_id, contract_pins [{contract_id, version}], governance_version, and last_validated. Wire system-factory scaffolding to generate this file for new repos. Add a CI step in spectrum-systems that validates ecosystem registry entries have accessible manifests conforming to the schema. Document the manifest requirement in docs/new-repo-checklist.md. Consider leveraging governance/repo-compliance.schema.json as the basis.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Implement Phase 1 governance enforcement: declared identity and contract pins

actions.title: Complete governance coverage for SYS-007, SYS-008, SYS-009
actions.description: Implementation boundary declarations and failure mode documentation are absent for SYS-007 (working-paper-review-engine), SYS-008 (docx-comment-injection-engine), and SYS-009 (spectrum-pipeline-engine). These systems are next in line for implementation. Governance gaps at implementation time become permanent architectural debt.
actions.recommended_action: Add SYS-007, SYS-008, SYS-009 implementation boundary declarations to docs/implementation-boundary.md following the template established for SYS-001 through SYS-006. Complete failure mode documentation in docs/system-failure-modes.md. Validate interface.md files meet depth requirements in docs/system-interface-spec.md. SYSTEMS.md schema references for SYS-007 and SYS-008 should point to contracts/schemas/ not contracts/examples/.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Complete governance coverage for SYS-007, SYS-008, SYS-009 before implementation

actions.title: Resolve external_artifact_manifest draft status
actions.description: The external_artifact_manifest contract carries 'draft' status in contracts/standards-manifest.json but is referenced in docs/contract-dependency-map.md as a consumer-facing contract for spectrum-pipeline-engine, study-artifact-generator, and comment-resolution-engine. SYS-008 (docx-comment-injection-engine) depends on this contract for binary artifact storage governance. Implementation against a draft contract is implementation against an unstable interface.
actions.recommended_action: Either promote external_artifact_manifest to 'stable' if the schema and examples are ready, or explicitly annotate dependent entries in the contract-dependency-map.md as 'pending contract stabilization' and block engine implementations from consuming it until promoted. Add a CI check that warns when a 'draft' contract appears in the dependency map as consumed by active engines.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Promote or formally block external_artifact_manifest contract (currently draft, consumed as stable)

actions.title: Wire review-to-issue automation to close the action tracker loop
actions.description: Design reviews produce action trackers (37+ items across three reviews) stored as markdown/JSON in docs/review-actions/. No automation converts these into GitHub issues. The ingest workflow exists but does not create tracked work. Action items are unassigned, untracked, and have unknown completion status.
actions.recommended_action: Create or extend a script that parses action tracker JSON files and creates GitHub issues using gh CLI or GitHub API. Each action with create_issue: true generates one issue with suggested_issue_title and suggested_labels. Wire to claude-review-ingest.yml workflow or create a separate workflow triggered by action tracker file additions to docs/review-actions/.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Automate GitHub issue creation from review action tracker files

actions.title: Define orchestration execution model via ADR
actions.description: spectrum-pipeline-engine (SYS-009) is the most architecturally sensitive repo in the ecosystem — it has the highest cross-repo coupling and depends on contracts from every other engine. Its execution model (synchronous vs. asynchronous, file-based vs. API-based, event-driven vs. polling, partial failure handling) is undefined. Implementation without this ADR will bake in ad hoc coupling decisions that are expensive to reverse.
actions.recommended_action: Create ADR-002 in architecture-decisions/ documenting the orchestration execution model decision. Evaluate options: (1) file-based sequential pipeline with manifest handoffs, (2) API-based orchestration with contract validation at each step, (3) event-driven with contract-typed message passing. Record the chosen model, alternatives considered, consequences, and integration constraints for upstream engines.
actions.suggested_issue_title: ADR: Define orchestration execution model for spectrum-pipeline-engine

actions.title: Resolve dual-track schema ambiguity (schemas/ vs contracts/schemas/)
actions.description: The repo maintains two schema registries with different naming conventions (kebab-case vs snake_case), different structural depth, and overlapping concerns. schemas/comment-schema.json coexists with contracts/schemas/comment_resolution_matrix.schema.json. SYSTEMS.md references schemas/ for SYS-001–004 and contracts/schemas/ for SYS-005–009. No CI validates consistency between tracks.
actions.recommended_action: Document explicit ownership rules: schemas/ holds base data lake field definitions (not consumed as artifact contracts), contracts/schemas/ holds the authoritative artifact contracts. Add CI to validate no field contradictions. Migrate all SYSTEMS.md schema references to use contracts/schemas/ consistently. Cross-reference shared types explicitly.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Clarify and enforce dual-track schema ownership (schemas/ vs contracts/schemas/)

actions.title: Create machine-readable contract consumer registry
actions.description: The standards-manifest.json includes intended_consumers per contract but there is no inverse registry mapping each engine to its pinned contract versions. When a contract bumps to a new version, there is no automated way to identify affected consumers or validate migration. docs/contract-dependency-map.md exists in prose only.
actions.recommended_action: Create consumer-registry.json (or extend ecosystem-registry.json) mapping each engine to pinned contract versions: {repo_name, contracts: [{artifact_type, pinned_version, status}]}. Generate from engine system-manifest.json files once Phase 1 exists. Add CI validating pinned versions against standards-manifest for compatibility.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Create machine-readable contract consumer registry for version impact analysis

actions.title: Implement constitutional release versioning
actions.description: Engine repos can pin to individual contract versions but cannot pin to a governance version packaging a consistent set of contracts, schemas, prompts, and standards together. When multiple contracts change between governance releases, engines face N separate migration decisions with no coordinated compatibility signal.
actions.recommended_action: Define a governance release process: tag governance-v<MAJOR>.<MINOR>.0 when the constitution reaches a stable state. Maintain a GOVERNANCE-CHANGELOG.md listing contract, schema, and prompt versions included in each governance release. Engine repos pin to governance releases rather than individual contract versions.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Define constitutional release versioning and governance changelog discipline

actions.title: Extend evaluation harness coverage to SYS-005 through SYS-009
actions.description: eval/test-matrix.md covers SYS-001 through SYS-004. SYS-005 through SYS-009 have evaluation.md files in systems/ directories but no corresponding eval/<system>/ directories with test cases, fixtures, or rubrics. Systems cannot be validated before implementation if there are no evaluation fixtures.
actions.recommended_action: Create eval/<system>/ directories for SYS-005 through SYS-009 with test-cases.md, fixtures/, and evaluation rubrics. Prioritize SYS-009 (pipeline engine) due to cross-cutting dependencies. Update eval/test-matrix.md to reflect full coverage.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Add evaluation harness coverage for SYS-005 through SYS-009

actions.title: Create ADRs for all architectural decisions since ADR-001
actions.description: Only ADR-001 (czar repo pattern) exists. Significant subsequent decisions lack ADR coverage: dual-track schema model, Python package introduction, evaluation coverage boundary (SYS-001–004 only), 9-system lifecycle model, agent role assignments, enforcement roadmap phasing. As the team grows, undocumented decisions will be relitigated.
actions.recommended_action: Write ADRs for: (1) dual-track schema model rationale, (2) evaluation harness coverage scope, (3) agent role separation model, (4) enforcement roadmap phasing, (5) orchestration execution model. Establish policy in CONTRIBUTING.md that interface-affecting decisions require ADRs.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Create ADRs for architectural decisions made since ADR-001

actions.title: Formalize system-factory as governance update propagation mechanism
actions.description: system-factory scaffolds new repos with governance primitives but the propagation path for governance updates to existing repos (new contract versions, updated prompts, schema changes) is undefined. As the constitution evolves, existing engines drift. The update model (push/pull/notification) has not been decided.
actions.recommended_action: Document the update propagation model: push (factory generates PRs to engine repos), pull (engine maintainers manually check for updates), or notification (ecosystem health check flags stale pins). Implement at least a notification mechanism before the second engine implementation begins.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Define and implement governance update propagation path for existing engine repos

actions.title: Implement ecosystem health signal
actions.description: No machine-readable ecosystem health summary exists. Maintainers cannot determine at a glance: which repos are compliant, which contracts have open migration debt, which reviews have unresolved actions, or which systems have evaluation gaps. Observability is manual review of markdown files.
actions.recommended_action: Create a CI job generating ecosystem-health.json aggregating: (1) registry completeness, (2) per-repo compliance status, (3) open action item count, (4) contract versions with pending consumers, (5) evaluation coverage by system. Commit on push to main or on a schedule.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Create automated ecosystem-health.json signal for governance observability

actions.title: Add schema backward-compatibility CI tests
actions.description: CONTRACT_VERSIONING.md declares semantic versioning where MINOR bumps must be backward compatible. This compatibility claim is untested. A MINOR bump could silently break existing consumers. The policy exists but is not mechanically enforced.
actions.recommended_action: Add a CI test that on contract schema changes loads the previous schema version from git history, runs existing example payloads against both old and new schema, and fails if an example valid under the old schema is invalid under the new schema.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Add backward-compatibility CI tests for contract schema MINOR version bumps

actions.title: Evaluate spectrum-program-advisor output contract consolidation
actions.description: spectrum-program-advisor has 7 output contracts (program_brief, study_readiness_assessment, next_best_action_memo, decision_log, risk_register, assumption_register, milestone_plan). This is the largest output surface of any system. Some of these contracts may be better modeled as components of a single program_state aggregate rather than independent artifacts. Without explicit scope constraints, the repo risks absorbing unbounded program management responsibilities.
actions.recommended_action: Review the 7 output contracts for consolidation opportunities. Evaluate whether decision_log, risk_register, assumption_register, and milestone_plan should be components of a single program_state contract. Document the decision as an ADR. Add explicit scope boundaries to the spectrum-program-advisor system overview.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Evaluate consolidation of spectrum-program-advisor output contracts

actions.title: Standardize meeting minutes contract format to JSON Schema
actions.description: The meeting minutes contract uses YAML (contracts/meeting_minutes_contract.yaml) while all other contracts use JSON Schema. This creates a different validation pathway and tooling requirement. Consistency in contract format reduces cognitive overhead and enables uniform validation tooling.
actions.recommended_action: Convert meeting_minutes_contract.yaml to JSON Schema format to match the other 16 contracts. Update references in SYSTEMS.md, docs/system-status-registry.md, and docs/implementation-boundary.md. If the YAML format serves a purpose (e.g., human readability for reviewers), document that rationale as an ADR.
actions.suggested_issue_title: Standardize meeting minutes contract to JSON Schema format for ecosystem consistency
